/home/mgalgs/aur-sleuth-data/bulk-reports/qwen-qwen3-235b-a22b-2507/aur-sleuth-futt8hi3/PKGBUILD: [SKIPPED] Skipping binary file: PKGBUILD

data.reasoning: Both models reviewed 0 files. The PKGBUILD — the single most important file to audit for any AUR package — was skipped by both with the message 'Skipping binary file: PKGBUILD'. This is a tooling misclassification: a PKGBUILD is a plain-text bash script, not a binary file. Because no file was actually analyzed, there is no basis to declare the package safe or unsafe. The verdict must be inconclusive pending a proper review.
data.disagreement_analysis: There is no substantive disagreement — both models produced identical skip results due to the same tooling defect (binary-file misdetection on the PKGBUILD). The 'shallow review' trigger reflects this shared failure, not a difference in judgment.
coverage_issues: PKGBUILD was never reviewed by either model — the core build/install logic is entirely unexamined.
No .install hooks, source files, or supplementary scripts were enumerated or reviewed because the audit aborted at the initial PKGBUILD check.
Both runs reported 0 prompt/completion tokens, confirming no actual LLM analysis occurred.
false_negatives: Any potential risk in the PKGBUILD (suspicious sources/URLs, sha sums set to SKIP, malicious build/package/prepare commands, curl|bash patterns, install scripts) is completely unassessed — not missed by reasoning but by total non-review.
re_audit_focus: PKGBUILD — force text-mode analysis; verify source array, integrity checks, and all build()/package()/prepare()/check() functions
Any .install file referenced by the PKGBUILD
Download URLs / source origins for the nvidia-settings beta binaries or tarball
learnings: The binary-file detection heuristic is misclassifying plain-text PKGBUILDs as binary, causing the initial safety gate to abort. PKGBUILD (and .install, .sh) should be force-treated as text regardless of byte-level heuristics.
A skip on the PKGBUILD specifically should escalate to 'inconclusive/manual-review-required' (as it did) but should also trigger an automatic retry with binary-detection disabled before giving up.
Add a guard: if files_reviewed == 0, never allow a 'safe' verdict; surface the tooling failure prominently rather than reporting 'No issues found.'
The 'No issues found.' line in the output is misleading when 0 files were reviewed — it should read 'No files reviewed; verdict undetermined.'
Detection of 0 tokens consumed is a reliable signal that the model never executed; the harness could use this to auto-flag the run as a non-audit.

data.reasoning: Both models failed to audit the PKGBUILD file due to incorrect binary file detection, leaving the entire package unaudited. Since PKGBUILD defines build/install behavior and may reference other scripts, this creates a critical coverage gap preventing safety assessment.
coverage_issues: PKGBUILD was skipped by both models due to erroneous binary file classification
No .install scripts or referenced source files were audited due to initial PKGBUILD skip
false_negatives: Potential risks in PKGBUILD functions (build(), package(), etc.) were completely missed
Possible .install script hooks or unsafe commands in source retrieval were not examined
learnings: Implement fallback text decoding for critical files like PKGBUILD when binary detection fails
Prioritize mandatory review of PKGBUILD before proceeding with other files
Add warning when critical files are skipped due to encoding issues
Improve binary detection heuristics to avoid false positives on shell scripts
